---
title: "MRW_Flow_May2026"
output: html_document
date: "2026-06-04"
author: "Colin G. Finlay"
---

# Setup

```{r Setup}
# Clear environment
rm(list=ls())

#use to set working directory 
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir="~/GitHub/FlowThrough_CoastalAg/analyses")
```

```{r Load packages}
require("tidyverse")
require("ggpubr")
require("ggtext")
require("xfun")
require("rstatix")
require("NSM3")
require("multcompView")
require("patchwork")
```

```{r Load data}
# Flow rates and water column heights
Flow_waterCols <- read.csv("../data/Hyde/MRW/MRW_FlowRates_LineIDs.csv")

# Fluxes calculated from MIMS data (Excel Smyth method):
MRW_MIMS_fluxes <- read.csv("../data/Hyde/MRW/MRW_fluxes_Rready_CF_20260712.csv")

# Teros 12 data from June and December 2025 (coordinates removed):
Teros12_MRW <- read.csv("../data/Hyde/MRW/CNH2_MRW_Design_and_Teros12.csv")
```

# Smallest and largest water column in Salt treatment

```{r}
# Largest water column
Flow_waterCols |>
  dplyr::filter(Tank == "salt") |>
  dplyr::slice_max(Average_height_cm, n = 1) |>
  dplyr::select(Core_ID, Tank, Average_height_cm) # 18_salt, 12.425 cm

# Smallest water column
Flow_waterCols |>
  dplyr::filter(Tank == "salt") |>
  dplyr::slice_min(Average_height_cm, n = 1) |>
  dplyr::select(Core_ID, Tank, Average_height_cm) # 24_salt, 8.9 cm
```

# Average flow rate per core

```{r}
# Calculate average flow per Core and place in new df
flow_avg <- Flow_waterCols |>
  dplyr::group_by(Core_ID) |>
  dplyr::summarise(avg_Flow_rate_L_hr = mean(Flow_rate_L_hr, na.rm = TRUE))

# Remove malformed row
flow_avg <- flow_avg |> dplyr::filter(Core_ID != "")

# Write out csv
#write.csv(flow_avg, "../csv_out/MRW_flow_avg.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

# Data wrangling

```{r format date times}
# date times:
MRW_MIMS_fluxes$Time <- as.POSIXct(MRW_MIMS_fluxes$Time, format = "%m/%d/%y %H:%M")
```

```{r split core IDs off of orig.ID}
MRW_MIMS_fluxes <- MRW_MIMS_fluxes %>%
  mutate(CoreID = str_extract(Orig_ID, "^[^_]+_[^_]+"))
```

# Plotting each timepoint

## µM Ar

```{r µM Ar}
plot.uM.Ar <- ggplot(data = MRW_MIMS_fluxes, aes(x = Time, y = uM_Ar)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ CoreID, nrow = 2) + 
  
  scale_x_datetime(
    breaks = unique(MRW_MIMS_fluxes$Time), 
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.15), 
    date_labels = "%b %d %H:%M" 
  ) +
  
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) + 
  labs(x = NULL, y = expression(paste("µM Ar"))) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    rect = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    plot.background = element_rect(color = NA),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
  
# View plot
plot.uM.Ar

# Save file
#ggsave("../figures/Hyde/MRW/Ar_conc.png", plot=plot.uM.Ar, device="png", path=NULL, scale=1, width=18.5, height=15, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```

## Ar flux

```{r Ar flux}
plot.Ar.flux <- ggplot(data = MRW_MIMS_fluxes, aes(x = Time, y = Ar_flux_umol_Ar_m.2_hr)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ CoreID, nrow = 2) + 
  
  scale_x_datetime(
    breaks = unique(MRW_MIMS_fluxes$Time), 
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.15), 
    date_labels = "%b %d %H:%M" 
  ) +
  
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) + 
  labs(x = NULL, y = expression(paste("µMol Ar m"^{-2}, " h"^{-1}))) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    rect = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    plot.background = element_rect(color = NA),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
  
# View plot
plot.Ar.flux

# Save file
#ggsave("../figures/Hyde/MRW/Ar_flux.png", plot=plot.Ar.flux, device="png", path=NULL, scale=1, width=18.5, height=15, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```

## N2-N flux

```{r N2-N flux}
plot.N2.flux <- ggplot(data = MRW_MIMS_fluxes, aes(x = Time, y = N2_flux_umol_N_m.2_hr)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ CoreID, nrow = 2) + 
  
  scale_x_datetime(
    breaks = unique(MRW_MIMS_fluxes$Time), 
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.15), 
    date_labels = "%b %d %H:%M" 
  ) +
  
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) + 
  labs(x = NULL, y = expression(paste("µMol N"[2], "-N m"^{-2}, " h"^{-1}))) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    rect = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    plot.background = element_rect(color = NA),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
  
# View plot
plot.N2.flux

# Save file
#ggsave("../figures/Hyde/MRW/N2_flux.png", plot=plot.N2.flux, device="png", path=NULL, scale=1, width=18.5, height=15, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```

## O2 flux

```{r O2 flux}
plot.O2.flux <- ggplot(data = MRW_MIMS_fluxes, aes(x = Time, y = O2_flux_umol_O2_m.2_hr)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ CoreID, nrow = 2) + 
  
  scale_x_datetime(
    breaks = unique(MRW_MIMS_fluxes$Time), 
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.15), 
    date_labels = "%b %d %H:%M" 
  ) +
  
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) + 
  labs(x = NULL, y = expression(paste("µMol O"[2], " m"^{-2}, " h"^{-1}))) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    rect = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    plot.background = element_rect(color = NA),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
  
# View plot
plot.O2.flux

# Save file
#ggsave("../figures/Hyde/MRW/O2_flux.png", plot=plot.O2.flux, device="png", path=NULL, scale=1, width=18.5, height=15, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```

# Drop outliers, average time points

```{r}
# Only clear outlier is 26_fresh T2. In this case, both N2 and O2 fluxes are outliers, so we'll drop the entire T2 for 26_fresh
MRW_MIMS_fluxes <- MRW_MIMS_fluxes %>%
  filter(Orig_ID != "26_fresh_L1_T2")
```

```{r average the time point for each core}
# Create new data frame with averaged values:
Core_Averages <- MRW_MIMS_fluxes %>%
  group_by(CoreID, Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_N2_Flux = mean(N2_flux_umol_N_m.2_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_O2_Flux = mean(O2_flux_umol_O2_m.2_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r set factors}
Core_Averages$Treatment <- as.factor(Core_Averages$Treatment)

# Rename treatments for plotting:
Core_Averages <- Core_Averages %>%
  mutate(
    Treatment = factor(Treatment, 
                       levels = c("Fresh", "Salt"), 
                       labels = c("Marsh Rd Creek", "Engelhard Boat Launch"))
  )

# Add a PairID for visualizing paired cores
Core_Averages <- Core_Averages %>%
  mutate(PairID = rep(1:(n() / 2), each = 2))
```

# Stats assumptions

```{r basic stat assumptions for single timepoint of flux data}
# Shapiro tests
  # N2 flux
Core_Averages %>%
  shapiro_test(Mean_N2_Flux)
  # Accept null: normally distributed

  # O2 flux
Core_Averages %>%
  shapiro_test(Mean_O2_Flux)
  # Accept null: normally distributed

# QQ plots
  # N2 flux
ggqqplot(Core_Averages, "Mean_N2_Flux") # Acceptable

  # O2 flux
ggqqplot(Core_Averages, "Mean_O2_Flux") # Acceptable
```

# Plotting average fluxes

## N2

```{r N2-N flux}
# Plot
plot.N2 <- ggplot(Core_Averages, aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_N2_Flux, color = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2, position = position_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0)) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("#009E73", "#0072B2"),
    labels = c("Marsh Rd Creek", "Engelhard Boat Launch"), 
    name = "Water Added to Soil"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1),
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x = element_markdown(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10), 
    legend.position = "top"
  ) + 
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "**Denitrification Rate**<br>(µmol N<sub>2</sub>-N m<sup>-2</sup> h<sup>-1</sup>)", 
    title = NULL
  )

# View plot
plot.N2

# Save plot
#ggsave("../figures/Ecosphere_pub/iris_tubes.tiff", plot=plot.IRIS_nested, device="tiff", path=NULL, scale=1, width= 18, height= 22, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```

```{r T test on N2 fluxes}
plot.N2_T_test <- ggplot(Core_Averages, aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_N2_Flux, color = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_line(aes(group = PairID), color = "gray50", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  
  # Added show.legend = FALSE to remove the "a"
  stat_compare_means(method = "t.test", paired = TRUE, 
                     label = "p.format", 
                     label.x.npc = "center",
                     show.legend = FALSE) + 
                     
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("#009E73", "#0072B2"),
    name = "Water Added to Soil"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1),
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x = element_markdown(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10), 
    legend.position = "right"
  ) + 
  labs(
    x = "**Water Added to Soil**",
    y = "**µmol N<sub>2</sub>-N m<sup>-2</sup> h<sup>-1</sup>**", 
    title = NULL
  )

plot.N2_T_test
```

## O2

```{r O2 flux}
# Plot
plot.O2 <- ggplot(Core_Averages, aes(x = Treatment, y = abs(Mean_O2_Flux), color = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2, position = position_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0)) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("#009E73", "#0072B2"),
    labels = c("Marsh Rd Creek", "Engelhard Boat Launch"), 
    name = "Water Added to Soil"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1),
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x = element_markdown(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10), 
    legend.position = "none"
  ) + 
  labs(
    x = "**Water Added to Soil**",
    y = "**Respiration Rate**<br>(µmol O<sub>2</sub> m<sup>-2</sup> h<sup>-1</sup>)", 
    title = NULL
  )

# View plot
plot.O2

# Save plot
#ggsave("../figures/Ecosphere_pub/iris_tubes.tiff", plot=plot.IRIS_nested, device="tiff", path=NULL, scale=1, width= 18, height= 22, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```

```{r T test on O2 fluxes}
plot.O2_T_test <- ggplot(Core_Averages, aes(x = Treatment, y = abs(Mean_O2_Flux), color = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_line(aes(group = PairID), color = "gray50", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  
  # Paired T test
  stat_compare_means(method = "t.test", paired = TRUE, 
                     label = "p.format", 
                     label.x.npc = "center",
                     show.legend = FALSE) + 
                     
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("#009E73", "#0072B2"),
    name = "Water Added to Soil"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1),
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x = element_markdown(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10), 
    legend.position = "right"
  ) + 
  labs(
    x = "**Water Added to Soil**",
    y = "**Respiration Rate**<br>(µmol O<sub>2</sub> m<sup>-2</sup> h<sup>-1</sup>)", 
    title = NULL
  )

plot.O2_T_test
```

## Arrange plots and save

```{r Stack plots using Patchwork}
# The / symbol means "stack vertically".
Plot.Denit_over_Resp <- plot.N2 / plot.O2

# View the plot
Plot.Denit_over_Resp

#ggsave("../figures/Hyde/MRW/Denit_over_Resp.png", plot=Plot.Denit_over_Resp, device="png", path=NULL, scale=1, width=18.5, height=15, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```


# Teros 12 data

```{r nicer date labels for plot}
# change format to e.g., 9-Jun
# factor and order so 9-Jun comes before 16-Dec

Teros12 <- Teros12 %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Convert the string to a real Date object
    real_date = mdy(Date_sampled), 
    
    # 2. Format to "Jun 9"
    # gsub("\\s+", " ", ...) looks for any instances of 1 or more spaces and reduces them to exactly 1 space
    Date_format = gsub("\\s+", " ", format(real_date, "%b %e")), 
    
    # 3. Make it a factor and automatically order it by the actual calendar date
    Date_format = fct_reorder(Date_format, real_date) 
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-real_date) # Drop the temporary date column
```

## Teros 12 stats

### Stat assumptions on residuals

```{r stats: moisture}
# 1. Fit models by group and extract residuals into a new column
Teros12 <- Teros12 %>%
  # Group by your date factor
  group_by(Date_format) %>%
  # Fit the model for each group and extract residuals
  mutate(
    model_residuals = residuals(lm(Moisture_m3_m3 ~ Sampling_Zone, data = pick(everything())))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 2. Run the Shapiro-Wilk test on the new residuals column per date
tapply(Teros12$model_residuals, Teros12$Date_format, shapiro.test)

# 3. Plot the residuals using ggplot2
ggplot(Teros12, aes(sample = model_residuals)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Date_format) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "QQ Plots of Model Residuals by Sampling Date",
    subtitle = "Model: Moisture ~ Sampling_Zone"
  )

# June 9: Not normal
# December 16: Normal
```

```{r stats: temperature}
# 1. Fit models by group and extract residuals into a new column
Teros12 <- Teros12 %>%
  # Group by your date factor
  group_by(Date_format) %>%
  # Fit the model for each group and extract residuals
  mutate(
    model_residuals = residuals(lm(Temperature_degC ~ Sampling_Zone, data = pick(everything())))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 2. Run the Shapiro-Wilk test on the new residuals column per date
tapply(Teros12$model_residuals, Teros12$Date_format, shapiro.test)

# 3. Plot the residuals using ggplot2
ggplot(Teros12, aes(sample = model_residuals)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Date_format) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "QQ Plots of Model Residuals by Sampling Date",
    subtitle = "Model: Temperature ~ Sampling_Zone"
  )

# June 9: Not normal
# December 16: Not normal
```

```{r stats: conductivity}
# 1. Fit models by group and extract residuals into a new column
Teros12 <- Teros12 %>%
  # Group by your date factor
  group_by(Date_format) %>%
  # Fit the model for each group and extract residuals
  mutate(
    model_residuals = residuals(lm(Conductivity_mS_cm ~ Sampling_Zone, data = pick(everything())))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 2. Run the Shapiro-Wilk test on the new residuals column per date
tapply(Teros12$model_residuals, Teros12$Date_format, shapiro.test)

# 3. Plot the residuals using ggplot2
ggplot(Teros12, aes(sample = model_residuals)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Date_format) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "QQ Plots of Model Residuals by Sampling Date",
    subtitle = "Model: Conductivity ~ Sampling_Zone"
  )

# June 9: Not normal
# December 16: Normal
```

## Teros 12 plots

### Moisture

#### Stats

```{r set factors}
# Sampling zone (plot)
Teros12$Sampling_Zone <- as.factor(Teros12$Sampling_Zone)
```

##### June 9, 2025

```{r Kruskal-Wallis}
kruskal_Moist_Jun9 <- kruskal.test(data = Teros12, Moisture_m3_m3 ~ Sampling_Zone, subset = Date_format == "Jun 9")

kruskal_Moist_Jun9
```

```{r SDCFlig}
# Subset by date
Teros12_Jun9 <- subset(Teros12, Date_format == "Jun 9")

# set random seed
set.seed(42)

# Pairwise comparisons
SDCFlig_Moist_Jun9 <- pSDCFlig(x = Teros12_Jun9$Moisture_m3_m3, g = Teros12_Jun9$Sampling_Zone, method = "Monte Carlo", n.mc = 10000)

SDCFlig_Moist_Jun9
```

##### December 16, 2025

```{r ANOVA with Tukey's HSD}
# First, subset data to only include the December 16 dates
Teros12_Dec16 <- subset(Teros12, Date_format == "Dec 16")

# Ensure Sampling_Zone is treated as a categorical factor
Teros12_Dec16$Sampling_Zone <- as.factor(Teros12_Dec16$Sampling_Zone)

# 1. Run the ANOVA
# This tests the overall hypothesis: is there ANY difference between the zones?
anova_model <- aov(Moisture_m3_m3 ~ Sampling_Zone, data = Teros12_Dec16)

# Check the ANOVA results (look for a Pr(>F) value less than 0.05)
summary(anova_model)

# 2. Run Tukey's HSD Post-Hoc
# If the ANOVA is significant, this tests every pair (A vs B, B vs C, A vs C)
tukey_results <- TukeyHSD(anova_model)

# View the pairwise p-values and confidence intervals
print(tukey_results)
```

```{r Multiple comparison letters}
# Generate the compact letter display based on the ANOVA and Tukey results
tukey_letters <- multcompLetters4(anova_model, tukey_results)

# Print the letters corresponding to your Sampling Zones
print(tukey_letters$Sampling_Zone)
```

##### Combined multiple comparisons for plot

```{r compact letter data frame}
# Create a data frame for the significance letters
significance_letters <- data.frame(
  Sampling_Zone = c("A", "B", "C", "A", "B", "C"), # Replace with your exact factor levels
  Date_format = as.factor(c("Jun 9", "Jun 9", "Jun 9", "Dec 16", "Dec 16", "Dec 16")),
  y_pos = c(0.7, 0.7, 0.7, 0.7, 0.7, 0.7), # Adjust these values to sit just above your highest whiskers/outliers
  Letter = c("a", "a", "b", "a", "a", "b")
)
```

#### Plotting

```{r Teros 12 plot: moisture}
plot.moisture <- ggplot(Teros12, aes(x=Sampling_Zone, y=Moisture_m3_m3)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(as.factor(Date_format)), nrow = 1, ncol = 2) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_point(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), size = 2, position = position_jitterdodge()) +
  
  # Text layer for significance letters
  # inherit.aes = FALSE prevents this layer from looking for 'Moisture_m3_m3' in new dataframe
  geom_text(data = significance_letters, 
            aes(x = Sampling_Zone, y = y_pos, label = Letter), 
            size = 5, 
            fontface = "bold", 
            color = "black", 
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  
  scale_color_manual(
    values=c("#86a8d2","#9c509f","#a88ec0"), 
    labels = c("A: Regulatory Floodway", "B: 1% Annual Chance", "C: 0.2% Annual Chance"), 
    name="Flood Hazard"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  # Remove plot grid lines
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) + 
  # Set axis title and text properties, tick marks, and labels
  theme(
    text=element_text(size=10), 
    axis.title=element_text(size=10,face="bold"),
    axis.text=element_text(size=10, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black",size=1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10), 
    legend.position="right", 
    legend.title = element_text(size=10), 
    legend.text=element_text(size=10)
  ) + 
  theme(
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"), 
    axis.title.y = element_markdown()
  ) + 
  labs(
    x = "", 
    y = "**Moisture (m<sup>3</sup> m<sup>-3</sup>)**", 
    title = ""
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  )

plot.moisture
```

### Temperature

#### Stats

```{r Kruskal-Wallis}
# June 9th, 2025
kruskal_Temp_Jun9 <- kruskal.test(data = Teros12_Jun9, Temperature_degC ~ Sampling_Zone)

kruskal_Temp_Jun9 # Significant

# December 16th, 2025
kruskal_Temp_Dec16 <- kruskal.test(data = Teros12_Dec16, Temperature_degC ~ Sampling_Zone)

kruskal_Temp_Dec16 # Significant
```

```{r SDCFlig}
# June 9th, 2025
# Pairwise comparisons
SDCFlig_Temp_Jun9 <- pSDCFlig(x = Teros12_Jun9$Temperature_degC, g = Teros12_Jun9$Sampling_Zone, method = "Monte Carlo", n.mc = 10000)

SDCFlig_Temp_Jun9

# December 16th, 2025
SDCFlig_Temp_Dec16 <- pSDCFlig(x = Teros12_Dec16$Temperature_degC, g = Teros12_Dec16$Sampling_Zone, method = "Monte Carlo", n.mc = 10000)

SDCFlig_Temp_Dec16
```

```{r compact letter data frame}
# Create a data frame for the significance letters
significance_letters_temp <- data.frame(
  Sampling_Zone = c("A", "B", "C", "A", "B", "C"), # Replace with your exact factor levels
  Date_format = as.factor(c("Jun 9", "Jun 9", "Jun 9", "Dec 16", "Dec 16", "Dec 16")),
  y_pos = c(29, 29, 29, 29, 29, 29), # Adjust these values to sit just above your highest whiskers/outliers
  Letter = c("a", "b", "b", "a", "a", "b")
)
```

#### Plotting

```{r Teros 12 plot: Temperature}
# Plotting:
plot.temp <- ggplot(Teros12, aes(x=Sampling_Zone, y=Temperature_degC)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(as.factor(Date_format)), nrow = 1, ncol = 2)+
                    geom_boxplot(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), outlier.shape = NA) +
                    geom_point(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), size = 2, position = position_jitterdodge()) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#86a8d2","#9c509f","#a88ec0"), labels = c("A: Regulatory Floodway", "B: 1% Annual Chance", "C: 0.2% Annual Chance"), name="Flood Hazard")+
  theme_bw() +
  #Remove plot grid lines
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) + 
  #Set axis title and text properties, tick marks, and labels
  theme(text=element_text(size=10), axis.title=element_text(size=10,face="bold"),
          axis.text=element_text(size=10, color = "black"),
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black",size=1),strip.text = element_text(size = 10), legend.position="right", legend.title = element_text(size=10), legend.text=element_text(size=10)) + 
theme(axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"), axis.title.y = element_markdown()) + 
  labs(x = "", y = "**Temperature (° C)**", title = "") +
  
    geom_text(data = significance_letters_temp, 
            aes(x = Sampling_Zone, y = y_pos, label = Letter), 
            size = 5, 
            fontface = "bold", 
            color = "black", 
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  theme(
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  )
 
plot.temp
```

### Conductivity

#### Stats

##### June 9, 2025

```{r Kruskal-Wallis}
kruskal_Cond_Jun9 <- kruskal.test(data = Teros12_Jun9, Conductivity_mS_cm ~ Sampling_Zone)

kruskal_Cond_Jun9 # Significant
```

```{r SDCFlig}
# Pairwise comparisons
SDCFlig_Cond_Jun9 <- pSDCFlig(x = Teros12_Jun9$Conductivity_mS_cm, g = Teros12_Jun9$Sampling_Zone, method = "Monte Carlo", n.mc = 10000)

SDCFlig_Cond_Jun9
```

##### December 16, 2025

```{r ANOVA with Tukey's HSD}
# 1. Run the ANOVA
# This tests the overall hypothesis: is there ANY difference between the zones?
anova_cond <- aov(Conductivity_mS_cm ~ Sampling_Zone, data = Teros12_Dec16)

# Check the ANOVA results (look for a Pr(>F) value less than 0.05)
summary(anova_cond) # p < 0.05

# 2. Run Tukey's HSD Post-Hoc
# If the ANOVA is significant, this tests every pair (A vs B, B vs C, A vs C)
tukey_cond <- TukeyHSD(anova_cond)

# View the pairwise p-values and confidence intervals
print(tukey_cond)
```

```{r Multiple comparison letters}
# Generate the compact letter display based on the ANOVA and Tukey results
tukey_letters_cond <- multcompLetters4(anova_cond, tukey_cond)

# Print the letters corresponding to your Sampling Zones
print(tukey_letters_cond$Sampling_Zone)
```

##### Combined multiple comparisons for plot

```{r compact letter data frame}
# Create a data frame for the significance letters
significance_letters_cond <- data.frame(
  Sampling_Zone = c("A", "B", "C", "A", "B", "C"),
  Date_format = as.factor(c("Jun 9", "Jun 9", "Jun 9", "Dec 16", "Dec 16", "Dec 16")),
  y_pos = c(0.35, 0.35, 0.35, 0.35, 0.35, 0.35), # Adjust these values to sit just above the highest whiskers/outliers
  Letter = c("a", "a", "b", "a", "a", "b")
)
```

#### Plotting

```{r Teros 12 plot: Conductivity}
# Plotting:
plot.cond <- ggplot(Teros12, aes(x=Sampling_Zone, y=Conductivity_mS_cm)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(as.factor(Date_format)), nrow = 1, ncol = 2)+
                    geom_boxplot(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), outlier.shape = NA) +
                    geom_point(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), size = 2, position = position_jitterdodge()) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#86a8d2","#9c509f","#a88ec0"), labels = c("A: Regulatory Floodway", "B: 1% Annual Chance", "C: 0.2% Annual Chance"), name="Flood Hazard")+
  theme_bw() +
  #Remove plot grid lines
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) + 
  #Set axis title and text properties, tick marks, and labels
  theme(text=element_text(size=10), axis.title=element_text(size=10,face="bold"),
          axis.text=element_text(size=10, color = "black"),
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0),panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black",size=1),strip.text = element_text(size = 10), legend.position="right", legend.title = element_text(size=10), legend.text=element_text(size=10)) + 
theme(axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"), axis.title.y = element_markdown()) + 
  labs(x = "Flood Hazard", 
    # Use standard markdown (**) for bold and HTML (<sup>) for superscripts
    y = "**Conductivity (mS cm<sup>-1</sup>)**", title = "") +
  
      geom_text(data = significance_letters_cond, 
            aes(x = Sampling_Zone, y = y_pos, label = Letter), 
            size = 5, 
            fontface = "bold", 
            color = "black", 
            inherit.aes = FALSE)
 
plot.cond
```

### 3 panel plot

```{r ggarrange Teros 12 data}
Plot.3panel.Teros12 <- ggarrange(plot.moisture, plot.temp, plot.cond, labels = c("(a)", "(b)", "(c)"), ncol = 1, nrow = 3, legend = "right", common.legend = T, align = "hv")

Plot.3panel.Teros12

#ggsave("../figures/Teros12_stats.png", plot=Plot.3panel.Teros12, device="png", path=NULL, scale=1, width=18, height=22, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```

## Teros 12 plots simplified for report

```{r convert moisture to percent}
Teros12$VWC_perc <- Teros12$Moisture_m3_m3 * 100
```


### Moisture

#### Plotting

```{r Teros 12 plot: moisture}
plot.moisture <- ggplot(Teros12, aes(x=Sampling_Zone, y=VWC_perc)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(as.factor(Date_format)), nrow = 1, ncol = 2) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_point(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), size = 2, position = position_jitterdodge(), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values=c("#86a8d2","#9c509f","#a88ec0"), 
    labels = c("A: Regulatory Floodway", "B: 1% Annual Chance", "C: 0.2% Annual Chance"), 
    name="Flood Hazard"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) + 
  theme(
    text=element_text(size=10), 
    axis.title=element_text(size=10),
    axis.text=element_text(size=10, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black",size=1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10), 
    legend.position="right", 
    legend.title = element_text(size=10), 
    legend.text=element_text(size=10)
  ) + 
  theme(
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"), 
    axis.title.y = element_markdown()
  ) + 
  labs(
    x = "", 
    y = "**% Soil Moisture**", 
    title = ""
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank()
  )

plot.moisture
```

### Temperature

#### Plotting

```{r Teros 12 plot: Temperature}
# Plotting:
plot.temp <- ggplot(Teros12, aes(x=Sampling_Zone, y=Temperature_degC)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(as.factor(Date_format)), nrow = 1, ncol = 2)+
                    geom_boxplot(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), outlier.shape = NA) +
                    geom_point(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), size = 2, position = position_jitterdodge(), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#86a8d2","#9c509f","#a88ec0"), labels = c("A: Regulatory Floodway", "B: 1% Annual Chance", "C: 0.2% Annual Chance"), name="Flood Hazard")+
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) + 
  theme(text=element_text(size=10), axis.title=element_text(size=10),
          axis.text=element_text(size=10, color = "black"),
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black",size=1),strip.text = element_text(size = 10), legend.position="right", legend.title = element_text(size=10), legend.text=element_text(size=10)) + 
theme(axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"), axis.title.y = element_markdown()) + 
  labs(x = "", y = "**Temperature**<br>(° C)", title = "") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank()
  )
 
plot.temp
```

### Conductivity

#### Plotting

```{r Teros 12 plot: Conductivity}
# Plotting:
plot.cond <- ggplot(Teros12, aes(x=Sampling_Zone, y=Conductivity_mS_cm)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(as.factor(Date_format)), nrow = 1, ncol = 2)+
                    geom_boxplot(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), outlier.shape = NA) +
                    geom_point(aes(color=Sampling_Zone), size = 2, position = position_jitterdodge(), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#86a8d2","#9c509f","#a88ec0"), labels = c("A: Regulatory Floodway", "B: 1% Annual Chance", "C: 0.2% Annual Chance"), name="Flood Hazard")+
  theme_bw() +
  #Remove plot grid lines
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) + 
  #Set axis title and text properties, tick marks, and labels
  theme(text=element_text(size=10), axis.title=element_text(size=10),
          axis.text=element_text(size=10, color = "black"),
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0),panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black",size=1),strip.text = element_text(size = 10), legend.position="right", legend.title = element_text(size=10), legend.text=element_text(size=10)) + 
theme(axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "cm"), axis.title.y = element_markdown(), axis.title.x = element_markdown()) + 
  labs(x = "**Flood Hazard**", 
    y = "**EC**<br>(mS cm<sup>-1</sup>)", title = "")
 
plot.cond
```

### 3 panel plot

```{r ggarrange Teros 12 data}
Plot.3panel.Teros12 <- ggarrange(plot.moisture, plot.temp, plot.cond, ncol = 1, nrow = 3, legend = "right", common.legend = T, align = "hv")

Plot.3panel.Teros12

#ggsave("../figures/Teros12_stats_SIMP.png", plot=Plot.3panel.Teros12, device="png", path=NULL, scale=1, width=18.5, height=20, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```

# End of Document

```{r session info}
# Reproducibility
sessioninfo::session_info()
```
